raw_input:
[4.0/5] Comfortable Head Band I work at home, so I can't get by without wearing a face mask for a good part of the day, but I do wear one if I am going out. I have had issues with some masks not staying over my ears. This is a good way to utilize those masks. The headband is comfortable and light weight. It has some stretch to it. The buttons are a little loose, so I tightened them before I wore the headband out in public. I found that I had to pay attention to how I was putting it on my head so the buttons were even, otherwise my mask was crooked. It's definitely a helpful item. [4.0/5] Works well for what I need Yeah, I have a physical job that requires I wear a mask while working. I work in an non-air-conditioned warehouse so I sweat...having a headband that can catch some of the sweat trying to drip into my eyes helps, but now with having to wear the mask, having the buttons on the headband allows me to combine the two instead of having to try to hook the mask's elastic behind my ears and then pull the headband over my ears and getting it situated on my forehead. Life has changed and finding the best way to work with the new normal is the only way to go...and this headband is working for me - maybe it will work to make it easier for others! [5.0/5] Amazing for mask use My wife loves this headband. Her masks hook up easy and this relieves all the ear strain the elastic bands cause. It is stylish and comfortable. Helps keep her quarantine hair (which is getting very long) manageable and out of her face. Fits great and works wonderful for all day use. [2.0/5] Nifty idea but I'd worry about it holding mask in place What's that saying about necessity being the mother of invention? Well here we are in a pandemic wearing face masks. Someone came up with the idea of a head band with buttons to hold the mask mask in place.

This is a thin material. Think very thin t shirt type material. The headband measures about 18 inches and just over 3 inches wide. I like the material for a headband. However, its use as a mask holder is iffy. Many people complain that the elastic that holds the masks in place makes their ears pop out. That is definitely true of some masks. They can also be wearying on the ears, having that elastic against your ears all day, so this headband is a great concept.
I put the headband on and as I stretched it over my head, I heard a pop, so some stitching gave somewhere.

I attached two types of masks, one at a time. First, it was a bit fiddly to get the elastic over the button. Once it just sprang off.

One mask using the buttons fit well. Another did not. The latter mask opened up on the sides.

I would hesitate to wear this out thinking that everything would stay put for hours. I like the headband as a headband. It's nice material. A sort of light grey merle, but if you are looking at this for long-term safety, I would say no. [5.0/5] comfy Very comfortable headband. 💕 [5.0/5] Great for hearing aid wearers I wear hearing aids and the over the ear masks get tangled up with my very expensive aids. If I added a pair of glasses, it is beyond uncomfortable. This band is the perfect solution I can protect my hearing aids, wear a mask, and my sunglasses. The ear loops on the mask attache to the buttons on either side of the headband. It also keeps the preparation on my forehead from rolling down my face. Thank you for this great design! [4.0/5] Hard to get the buttons positioned just right on your head Everybody has a different size head diameter and their ears are positioned differently. I let my nurse sister try this out in the ER where she has to wear a mask during her entire 12 hr shift. She said this band most definitely takes some of the pressure off the back of her ears but she ended up remodeling it by removing the buttons and placing them in a better spot for her head. This is not a design flaw, it is simply that everyone has slightly different dimensions and when you are trying to take the pressure off the back off your ears with mask straps you may need to tweak the buttons a bit. All in all, I recommend. [5.0/5] It works! Such a simple thing, really. But for those who have to wear masks all day every day this is a real game changer. It’s easy, and comfortable, to wear. It gives the ears a break (a real thing...especially for glasses wearers). It has the capability to stretch a bit, so should fit many different head sizes. I love it! (just hope it lasts!) [5.0/5] Works, lightweight, and comfortable - my face is just too small for use with masks This is a great little headband for securing your facemask if you've either worn a rash on your ears due to bands or just need it a little tighter on your face for a better seal. The little buttons are very secure on the headband and to me don't seem in danger of popping off. The cloth is very soft and firm on the head so it stays in place pretty well through the day and isn't super thick so those in hotter climates won't be sweating through terribly and soaking the band.

I'm giving this 5 stars because it DOES do what it's supposed to (stays on the head, secures bands around the buttons, etc) and feels good and soft but sadly my head/face is just too petite for this to work with a mask so I'm having to stick with my children's sized masks or the ones that go around the back of my head to ensure I don't have gapping around my cheeks or chin. The headband does it's job, masks are just too big/wide for me to actually have them work together.

So it IS a very good product, it's just not enough to solve my personal issue and that's not a fault of the headband so it would be unfair to rate it poorly because of that. In all likelihood for the vast majority of people, this will work perfectly

However, I still use it as a plain headband! I like that it's so firmly in place that I can use it to hold my hair back when I wash my face and do my skincare routine and works great when I'm excercising and don't want a mess of curls flying into my sweaty face. So it's still serving a great purpose for me even if I can't use it with masks! [1.0/5] Nope Already donated this item. Obviously needing to wear face masks everyday right now had me intrigued by this headband but this one did not live up to what I was hoping it would be. It's material was super thin. When I would wear my mask it would pull the headband down and the whole thing just did not work. Don't waste your time unless maybe you have a child that could use it. Average (I think) size woman's head did not work. [3.0/5] doesn't work for me I have sewn reusable masks that I wear. I do not wear disposable masks. I think this was designed with disposables in mind. My masks have thicker elastics band that the button just can't hold well. I think the lighter weight disposables would do better with this headband. It was a nice idea but it did not work. I haven't decided if I am going to snip off the buttons and try and use it as a regular headband or if I will pass this on to someone else. [4.0/5] Mask Headband... I wore this for the first time today during a trip to the grocery store and it was perfect. I usually can't wear these types of headbands because due to the shape of my head they always just slide right off my head.

The buttons served a dual purpose for me...they kept the mask on my face comfortably without the bands digging into the backs of my ears, and the mask, in turn, kept the headband on my head...can't argue with that! It's soft and comfortable and I'm very happy with it. I just wish it came in more colors. [5.0/5] Must have... The material is soft and very comfortable to wear. I've tried many infinity headbeads that would always roll off my head, but this actually stays put! That alone has me excited! It it snug enough to stay put, but isn't so tight it gives you a headache. The placement of the buttons falls just behind the ears so they are perfect to wear with a mask and keep your ears from hurting. [5.0/5] Love it It's awesome [4.0/5] Nice product I used this the whole day today.

Pros: Soft material and it’s very comfortable to wear. I used a disposable face mask and the loops pretty much stayed in place.

Con (for me): after an hour, the headband kept on slipping from my head [5.0/5] This headband is great! When I ordered this I completely forgot that it came with buttons for face masks. I opened it, was confused, then remembered and tried it on with a mask. It works so well! it doesn't pull the mask too much tighter but it takes the strain off your ears. Without the mask it is super cute too, not too thick so it would be good for all weather. [1.0/5] Terrible so I got this headband and just like the other one from amazon buttons popped off I'm so upset no that's not just a headband it had buttons before I wore it with my mask then the buttons popped off [1.0/5] Broke first day It broke after I wore it once [4.0/5] Works It is a soft material headband and it is comfortable to wear. You do need to make sure the headband is flat against your head or your mask elastic will keep popping off the buttons though. It does give your ears a break if you have to wear a mask all day. I wish the buttons were more firmly sewn in place though.

response:
Headband for holding face mask. Most people like it. Here what cave people say:

GOOD THINGS: Soft, comfortable material. Lightweight. Has stretch, fit many head sizes. Buttons on sides hold mask elastic so ears not hurt all day. Good for glasses wearers, hearing aid wearers. Stays on head better than other headbands. Helps keep hair back too. Sweat catcher for warehouse workers.

BAD THINGS: Buttons sometimes loose, may need tighten before use. Hard to position buttons in right spot for every head. Some buttons pop off. One broke first day. Headband slip off some heads after hour. Not work well with thick elastic on reusable masks, better for disposable masks. Small face people have trouble, mask gap at cheeks. One person hear stitching pop when stretch over head.

OVERALL: Most reviewers happy, give 4 or 5 stars. Good idea, solve real problem during pandemic. But quality not always consistent. Buttons main weak point. Nurse sister had to move buttons to better position for her head. Simple fix but annoying.
